tweets.text: And if you’re a #smallbiz owner looking to increase exports, learn about the Department’s Direct Line program, which allows small to mid-size companies to engage with U.S. ambassadors on investment and export opportunities overseas. #shopsmall https://t.co/4dked6DqOF
The work Emma’s Torch does exemplifies the best of America, with successive generations coming from somewhere else and becoming part of the story of our country and carrying it forward.

On #SmallBusinessSaturday, we celebrate innovative U.S. businesses like Emma's Torch, where local citizens teach refugees culinary skills and help with job placement in the U.S. Local businesses help drive the global economy and address the needs of neighborhoods and communities. https://t.co/8b214n0UDy

The @G7 has set us up for success on issues that matter to the citizens of each of our countries and that matter to people around the world. Our greatest strategic asset is our ability to come together and work in common cause. – @SecBlinken https://t.co/zjIqxEI4rK

During World War II, the U.S. Marines turned to the Navajo Nation to protect Allied military plans. On #NativeAmericanHeritageDay, learn how the the Code Talkers created the only unbreakable code in modern military history: https://t.co/3gCAEkBlvV

On Thanksgiving Day, Americans show their appreciation for life’s blessings by enjoying home-cooked feasts with family and close friends. @ShareAmerica explains the traditions and events of #Thanksgiving week: https://t.co/knHDEd69gP

From everyone here at the State Department, wishing you and your loved ones a happy Thanksgiving. May your table be bountiful and surrounded by warmth, friends, and family. 🦃 🥧 https://t.co/1YNSh3lntE
That concludes the Secretary’s travel to Italy, which included his final G7 meetings as Secretary of State. Arrivederci! https://t.co/r9CriOfHDb

Our Tri-Mission Italy team includes @AmbasciataUSA, @USinHolySee, and @USUNRome. At a time of incredible challenges, our teams are finding important ways to work and cooperate with partners around the world. We thank them for their extraordinary work on behalf of our country. https://t.co/jwqOuxcODm
@SecBlinken The Secretary also met with Pope Francis, expressing gratitude for his global leadership in protecting the most vulnerable and promoting human dignity. https://t.co/tYZZHbQPvF

The U.S. partners closely with the Holy See on a range of global issues.

During @SecBlinken’s meeting with Holy See officials, they discussed the recent Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, shared commitment to Ukraine's sovereignty, and promoting democracy in Nicaragua and Venezuela. https://t.co/DZ9lu4XzFu
On @SecBlinken’s last day of travel to Italy and the Holy See, he met with @Pontifex as well as our Tri-Mission Italy teams. All the details on today’s events in the 🧵. https://t.co/fEYr364r37

.@G7 partners pledged steadfast support for Ukraine into and through 2025, continuing what has been a truly unprecedented partnership – sharing the burden, determined to do everything that we can to help Ukraine deal with ongoing Russian aggression. – @SecBlinken https://t.co/vinKhglM6o

.@SecBlinken ended his day with a press conference. He noted that when we work together in common purpose, we can better the lives of people everywhere. For example, the G7 in recent years has boosted global investment in food security, sustainable infrastructure, & clean energy. https://t.co/s7Z5K0UPB2
Indo-Pacific partners then joined the discussion, with sessions focused on cooperating with the G7 to address global challenges and uphold a free and open Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/Gkm4E09Y8L

The Secretary often meets one-on-one with his counterparts. These meetings are referred to as bilats, short for bilateral meeting. @SecBlinken had two bilats today: one with Italian Foreign Minister Tajani and another later in the day with Indian Foreign Minister Jaishankar. https://t.co/bT8EndkDTp

The Secretary and the G7 foreign ministers welcomed Ukrainian Foreign Minister @andrii_sybiha for talks. The U.S. and our G7 partners remain steadfast in our support for Ukraine 🇺🇦. https://t.co/qbWssKa9pl
It’s Day 2 at G7 Italia 🇮🇹 with @SecBlinken. Here’s what the Secretary was up to today. 🧵 https://t.co/BmqgTgphtQ

As the G7 President, Italy has hosted 24 G7 meetings in 2024. U.S. Embassy in Italy staffer Noemi takes us along as @SecBlinken attends the last day of the last G7 ministerial meeting in Italy. https://t.co/YL0AtjxDze
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Italy. https://t.co/iwPklb0NgD  https://t.co/F3eli37fOB
Next, the G7 ministers welcomed Arab partners for a session on how they can work together to advance stability in the Middle East. https://t.co/2sWBiXDR9j

As the G7 president, Italy has hosted 24 G7 meetings this year, including the Leaders Summit. While in the historic town of Anagni, the ministers visited a medieval cathedral in between their sessions. https://t.co/Kiv0vULPNg
@WFP In the afternoon, @SecBlinken met with G7 foreign ministers to discuss tackling pressing global issues, including the situations in the Middle East and Red Sea. https://t.co/J785XDHCzP

The Secretary began his day with a stop at @WFP headquarters in Rome. He thanked the World Food Programme for being a partner in addressing food insecurity and their work to prevent conflict. https://t.co/nC43PLaDp6
Yesterday, @SecBlinken began his trip to Italy, with stops in Rome, Anagni, and Fiuggi to engage with @G7 partners. Here’s a recap:⤵️ https://t.co/rVIz8fgAPe

The U.S. and our @G7 partners want people everywhere to thrive. That means helping those in emerging markets develop what they need to participate in the global economy. And it's why the G7 launched the Partnership for Global Infrastructure and Investment. https://t.co/GdUomQ9GHJ

This week at State, @SecBlinken travels with @POTUS to the Amazon and then to Rio de Janeiro for his engagements at the #G20Brazil. And the Department releases the Open Doors Report and celebrates International Education Week. https://t.co/bIkzaHrdUg

The number of international students studying in the United States topped 1.1 million in the 2023–2024 academic year — an all-time high. More from @ShareAmerica on how these students are contributing to the U.S. economy: https://t.co/RfJuXmMwOM

Currently, 26M people in North Korea are being subjected to human rights abuses that deserve freedom and dignity. Minnie Mills partnered with us to uplift the stories of North Korean escapees like Evelyn, who are giving a voice to those who don’t have one inside the DPRK. https://t.co/aCdRaa4lfv
These numbers are growing every day. They’ve grown every year. They tell an incredibly important story: Foreign direct investment may be the best measure of trust and confidence in the future.

APEC economies have invested $1.7 trillion in the United States – from clean energy to semiconductors – supporting 2.3 million American jobs. Meanwhile, U.S. businesses invested over $1.4 trillion into APEC economies, the largest source of foreign direct investment within APEC.

.@SecBlinken shares how investment in Asia-Pacific economies is building trust and confidence. “Help us continue to shape a future that brings more people together, that’s more inclusive, that’s more sustainable, that’s more open, that builds greater opportunity.” #APECPeru https://t.co/PrOOIWfvOo

.@APEC Economic Leaders, including @POTUS, met in Lima last week to advance priorities on trade and investment, digitalization and innovation, and sustainable and inclusive growth. Learn more about the outcomes from #APECPeru2024: https://t.co/NXPrZL8s67 https://t.co/a53Q602s9j

Our passport team is working hard to make passport renewals easier. 🛂

@SecBlinken stopped by @travelgov’s D.C. passport agency to meet the people who helped make this a record-breaking year for passports (over 24M passports issued) and launch online U.S. passport renewals. https://t.co/5nmznqSNAh

The partnership between India and the U.S. shows the world how cooperation, innovation, and community engagement can preserve critical natural resources upon which the planet depends. https://t.co/JYsu4ludIR

India and the U.S. are working together to combat pollution in our great rivers. Thousands of miles apart, we are united by a common cause to protect the rivers that have shaped our cultures and countries. https://t.co/RN11hUCNlT

On Sunday, @POTUS was the first sitting U.S. President to visit the Amazon.

He announced that the United States has delivered record climate financing to developing countries, surpassing the $11 billion per year target he pledged in 2021. https://t.co/npNYGMhAco

During International Education Week, we caught up with some international students at @GeorgeMasonU. From language learning to creating new connections, discover why studying abroad matters to them. #IEW24 #StudyWithUS https://t.co/PTem6SrMmd

We are ensuring Americans’ economic security through our work with allies and partners on these and other crucial issues.

Economic Diplomacy plays a critical role in accomplishing our top foreign policy priorities. 6/6 https://t.co/Wx5lyc4jMN

Addressing the climate crisis not only helps the planet, but also improves the economic security of Americans and people around the world. As part of U.S. efforts, we negotiated the first @UN Global Stocktake and secured emissions targets in aviation and shipping sectors. 5/6 https://t.co/Ke3OQaNcOH

For global supply chains in critical minerals essential to the energy transition, we want to avoid dependence on a single source.

The U.S. launched a Minerals Security Partnership with 14 countries, the EU, and the private sector to build reliable supply chains. 4/6 https://t.co/BRgVtjWmtK

Foreign investment also drives growth in the U.S. and addresses global challenges. 
 
Korean solar cell manufacturers invested more than $2.5B to build factories in Georgia projected to create 2,500 green jobs. 3/6 https://t.co/KI7uFH2mSa

State is an essential gateway for American businesses operating overseas.

Through our Direct Line program, hundreds of U.S. companies have connected to our ambassadors and experts on the ground in more than 190 countries to gain critical market insights. 2/6 https://t.co/fi2oOzrOvz

The rare metals in your phone rely on global supply chains. Disruptions caused by shocks like the COVID-19 pandemic revealed how important it is that we advance resilient supply chains.

Addressing this issue is part of our Economic Diplomacy, led by @State_E offices. 🧵 1/6 https://t.co/E4tPJHv8hh

Responsible AI use has the potential to help solve urgent challenges while making our world more prosperous, productive, innovative, and secure. That's why we have convened key member countries for the inaugural meeting of the International Network of AI Safety Institutes. https://t.co/ruAsdbV2SP

We are working together with our partners across the globe to pave the way for a sustainable future. Through academic exchanges with India, we are working together to find solutions for agricultural challenges in the face of climate change. https://t.co/YVvnTJjCd6 https://t.co/OeDOXC2xWG

.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the Overseas Security Advisory Council Annual Briefing in Arlington, Virginia.
https://t.co/1WbWtIftzP https://t.co/BZWhGRyG3W

The U.S. has delivered on @POTUS’s pledge to scale up support to $11 billion per year for developing countries’ efforts to protect vital ecosystems, transition to clean energy, and bolster their climate resilience. https://t.co/sf0dQodQE8

With @USAID, we’re implementing the President’s Emergency Plan for Adaptation and Resilience to help 500 million people worldwide adapt to and manage the impacts of climate change by 2030. We are delivering $3 billion per year toward this commitment. https://t.co/F1or9ScMmN

The U.S. is doing our part at home to build on historic agreements at previous UN Climate Change Conferences, including working to reduce emissions of methane and other climate super pollutants. We’re also investing in clean energy and green jobs. https://t.co/suAi60QSMW
At the UN Climate Change Conference, the United States has worked with international partners to accelerate global climate action. 🧵 https://t.co/I5MUgO7D5Y

Of the world’s top 100 places to start a business, one-third are in the United States. This #GlobalEntrepreneurshipWeek, learn more about the thousands of Americans driving a U.S. entrepreneurship boom: https://t.co/OFzFpxF0oo

At the #G20 Summit in Brazil this week, 19 member countries, plus the African Union and the European Union, unveiled new initiatives to address geopolitical and socioeconomic challenges. Learn more about what @POTUS announced:

From the depths of the ocean floor to the galaxies of outer space, our Bureau of Oceans and International Environmental and Scientific Affairs has made a worldwide impact. As @SciDiplomacyUSA celebrates 50 years, here is a look at their major achievements: https://t.co/s4MDgrjT9A

Happy International Education Week! As @SecBlinken shared today, "the State Department and our partners are committed to expanding opportunities to study in the United States – and to diversifying the ranks of students we send overseas." #IEW2024 https://t.co/zJX8B8jZf7

Over 1.1 million students from around the world studied in the United States last year, contributing $50 billion to U.S. campuses and communities. Explore the #OpenDoorsReport released today on international educational exchange ➡️ https://t.co/4C9JXeJV0f. #IEW2024 https://t.co/KxuXmbYlTY

This week at State, @SecBlinken travels to Belgium to discuss support for Ukraine with @NATO, European Union, and Ukrainian leaders; @POTUS and the Secretary advance Asia-Pacific trade and investment at #APECPeru2024; and climate talks kick off at #COP29 in Azerbaijan. https://t.co/Ywsjmmx5dx

In the leadup to @POTUS’ travel to the G20 Leaders Summit in Rio de Janeiro, U.S. Global Music Ambassador @breland was in Brazil for a G20 Culture Ministerial side event. There, he shared how he’s using music as a positive force in the world and encouraging others to do the same. https://t.co/plC36O8NEo
Trade with APEC economies is increasing prosperity for American businesses and farmers. Learn more about how APEC supports your future at https://t.co/lPdsHM90iU. https://t.co/9NMTBWMob0

Our public and private sectors continue to deliver for APEC economies, catalyzing sustainable, high-quality investments that create good jobs within APEC member economies – including the United States. https://t.co/DGALtZZ4n2
Through APEC, partnership is easier, leading to real jobs and stronger economies. These partnerships have facilitated over $50 billion in investments in American businesses. https://t.co/dNcNgg8fgD

Investments from American companies shore up resilient supply chains, bolster regional health and cyber preparedness, and support small- and medium-sized businesses across APEC economies. Learn more at: https://t.co/FZscLMVHzV https://t.co/VnH1xF08qy
APEC initiatives give small businesses in the U.S. access to new tools and funding, creating opportunities for these companies to go global. 🌏 https://t.co/UYDBp4xPr4
The United States supports predictable, fair trade rules that lower costs and promote trade and investment at home and across the Asia-Pacific. https://t.co/OFYvz1xg3k
The conversations happening at #APECPeru2024 drive real benefits for Americans. Here’s how Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation economies invest in American communities: ⤵️ https://t.co/pchNXYUxQr

.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at a CEO Summit on the margins of the APEC Summit in Lima, Peru. #APECPeru2024
https://t.co/WyNdf6OsZF  https://t.co/JFtR0WfQZi

The United States is committed to supporting a Black Sea region that is peaceful, secure, prosperous, and interconnected. By investing in renewable energy and regional connectivity, Black Sea nations are building their own clean energy future. #BlackSeaCleanEnergy #COP29 https://t.co/RQKHKIlztP
.@APECPeru gets underway in Lima this week. Here are three things you should know about how fair and inclusive trade across the Asia-Pacific region impacts you. #APECPERU2024 https://t.co/xonuf852CB
At @NATO Headquarters, @SecBlinken talks about how the best way to defend our countries, to prevent wars, and to have security is through our alliances and partnerships. https://t.co/QU69s1zK6I

The quickest way to end Putin’s war of aggression against Ukraine is simple: stop those who are enabling and fueling the Kremlin’s aggression and demand a just peace that upholds the principles of the United Nations Charter. https://t.co/Z04cliGAUO
The Secretary’s day concluded with back-to-back meetings with his British and EU counterparts.
The Secretary then met with Ukrainian Foreign Minister Sybiha where he reiterated the United States’ commitment to continuing military and economic assistance to Ukraine. https://t.co/rPdUVIhriv

Next, @SecBlinken participated in a meeting of the North Atlantic Council, reaffirming our ironclad commitment to Euro-Atlantic security.

Here’s more from NATO on what the council is and why it’s important: https://t.co/6NINPwnjZY https://t.co/lrlyRgzqQA

The Secretary began his day at @NATO headquarters. He met with @SecGenNATO Rutte to underscore our commitment to the NATO Alliance. The United States stands #UnitedWithUkraine in supporting Ukraine’s sovereignty and territorial integrity. https://t.co/dSsYnZKGZx

.@SecBlinken wrapped his visit to Belgium today.
Here’s a recap of his day in Brussels. https://t.co/2lcQTijNTo

These ships will be national assets that ensure access to both polar regions – and will be capable of executing key Coast Guard missions, including defense readiness; marine environmental protection; ports, waterways, and coastal security; and search and rescue.

This collaboration will bolster the shipbuilding industry and industrial capacity of each nation; build closer ties among our countries through information exchange and workforce development; and uphold our commitment to security and prosperity in the polar regions.

Let’s break the ice … so ships can get through the Arctic and Antarctic regions.

Our new Icebreaker Collaboration Effort (ICE Pact) with Canada and Finland will build more modern icebreaker ships to facilitate trade, strengthen supply chains, and create good-paying jobs at home.

We’re continuing our work to strengthen the @NATO Alliance that is so critical to the security of the United States and security of countries throughout Europe. That includes our ongoing support for Ukraine in the face of an ongoing Russian aggression. https://t.co/ly8bleWL5a

Local staff are the backbone of our missions around the world. They are bridges to the communities we serve and contribute the expertise necessary to our success. Thank you for all you do. #FSNDay https://t.co/InOgHUxqeJ
